topology.summary: One declared UE follows one named source-to-target handover path across two named cells and two named DUs.

blast_radius.max_change_scope: one declared handover path across two named cells and two named DUs
blast_radius.operator_impact: A bad change may strand the declared UE on the named handover path, but it must not imply broad roaming or generalized mobility support.
blocked_until: source and target readiness are both reviewable
path-switch state is visible before cutover
rollback can restore the pre-handover serving path or record a blocked state

required_checks: the source and target cells match the profile
handover sequencing and path-switch checks are named explicitly
reviewers can inspect pre-handover and post-handover evidence separately

restore_actions: restore the pre-handover serving path for the declared UE
capture a blocked-state incident bundle if the serving path cannot be restored cleanly
rollback_triggers: handover preparation or path switch loses auditability
post-handover verify fails for the declared UE

success_criteria: pre-handover and post-handover serving state are both reviewable
rollback evidence can explain whether the source path was restored

manual_review: confirm the repo still treats mobility as future-lane proof work rather than current support

non_claims: no broad multi-cell parity claim outside the declared handover path
no broad multi-DU parity claim outside the declared handover path
no broad topology parity claim outside the declared profile
